integrity: sha256:f2348111566b9d80eb1260ae8a5fc5b2a433d970fc8035c43c1cb5414c0fde59
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've reviewed the full implementation against all four acceptance criteria — `waive.go` (ac-1/ac-2), `waiverauthor.go`/`waivers.go` (render + fold seam), `waiverstale.go` + `audit.go` + `store/manifest.go` AuditConfig (ac-3), and `vocabprose_test.go` (ac-4) — along with the `classifyPair` seam, path/slug helpers, and the lifecycle/e2e tests.\n\nThe implementation faithfully matches the spec's intent on every criterion: the create/reaffirm exit contract and refusal wording, self-validation of rendered bytes, verbatim owner copy and fresh frozen stamps, the marker-delimited append-only reaffirmation log, the status-driven fold with wall-clock lapse confined to ephemeral audit stdout, the separate never-merged waiver count with a strictly-greater threshold defaulting to 3 (consistent with `SpecStale`'s `\u003e` operator and default), and the model-derived verb-word witness with a genuine RED/GREEN mutation proof. I found no semantic divergence that the mechanical checks miss.\n\n{\"findings\":[]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-22, commit: 45b3d53fa9f694463ea8cfd5af04d220b4251dee }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/verb-surfaces@45b3d53fa9f694463ea8cfd5af04d220b4251dee, spec/verb-surfaces@fc77362bd91d5e77b199e22ddd22bd272132e4f7], digest: sha256:9fd49eb528183e776be218783941e8f7bf85c68bce07f796a6ab013585d3a29b, integrity: sha256:f2348111566b9d80eb1260ae8a5fc5b2a433d970fc8035c43c1cb5414c0fde59, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
